title: thymus munbyanus subsp. mastigophorus
intro: Thymus munbyanus subsp. mastigophorus, commonly known as Mastigophorous thyme, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which help in treating respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The therapeutic actions of Mastigophorous thyme include expectorant, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which aid in relieving coughs, reducing inflammation, and calming the nervous system. Bioactive constituents of this plant include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as thymol, carvacrol, and rosmarinic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
